assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the 'Version Impact Table' (lines 49-57) shows all five versions (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) with 'NO' in the 'Affected?' column. Each ships serde_json >= 1.0.135 (either 1.0.138 or 1.0.139), which is outside the CVE's affected range of < 1.0.135. The summary on line 59 states: 'Result: NO supported versions in the 2.2.x stream are affected.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 7 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, line 5 states: 'Triage Decision: Case C -- Close as Not a Bug (no supported versions affected)'. Section '2. Transition to Closed with resolution "Not a Bug"' (lines 48-52) proposes: 'jira.transition_issue(TC-8002, status="Closed", resolution="Not a Bug")' with rationale referencing 'Case C from the triage-security skill'. Line 76 explicitly states: 'No remediation tasks created. Case C closes the issue -- no fix is needed because no supported versions are affected.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, section '1. Add comment documenting version impact evidence' (lines 25-46) contains a proposed comment that lists each version with its serde_json version: 2.2.0 (1.0.138, NO), 2.2.1 (1.0.138, NO), 2.2.2 (retag of 2.2.1 1.0.138, NO), 2.2.3 (1.0.139, NO), 2.2.4 (1.0.139, NO). The comment concludes: 'All supported versions in the 2.2.x stream ship serde_json >= 1.0.135, which is outside the affected range (< 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 7 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, section '3. Set VEX Justification' (lines 56-60) proposes setting the VEX Justification to 'Component not Present' via: 'jira.edit_issue(TC-8002, fields={"customfield_12345": "Component not Present"})'. The rationale states: 'The vulnerable version of serde_json (< 1.0.135) is not shipped in any supported release.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 7 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, the 'Actions NOT Taken' section (lines 74-78) explicitly states: 'No remediation tasks created. Case C closes the issue -- no fix is needed because no supported versions are affected.' The Steps Completed table (line 91) confirms: 'Case C: Close as Not a Bug. No remediation tasks needed.' No remediation task creation actions appear anywhere in any of the three output files.
